source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/04_2G & 3G (A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW)/▶ CE MDR/230907 기술문서 1차 보완 리뷰 (with 컨설팅)/참고 - 기술문서 & 임상평가 NC Report.xlsx

sourceSummary.oneLine: NC report listing technical documentation and clinical evaluation review comments for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detector CE MDR files.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as a traceable list of consultant or reviewer nonconformities and requested documentation corrections for EU MDR technical documentation.
keyPoints: The report states that corrections are needed by 09.30.
The product description identifies A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW as wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors for general radiographic diagnosis, not for mammography.

Multiple comments request MDR-aligned updates, including classification reconsideration under Rule 10, UDI carrier information, CE/Notified Body number handling, labeling symbols, residual risk disclosure, and clinical benefit measurability.
The reviewer asks to use ISO 15223-1:2021 and MDCG 2020-5 for equivalence, and to include revision status for supporting documents.
The source includes repeated concerns about inconsistent addresses across product description, label, Declaration of Conformity, and manufacturing information.

evidence.claim: The NC report requires corrections by 09.30.
evidence.evidenceText: - 30일 (09.30) 까지 보완 필요

evidence.claim: A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW are described as wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors

evidence.claim: The detectors support auto exposure detection technology without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of adult and child human anatomy.
evidence.evidenceText: indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Intended Purpose

evidence.claim: The product is not to be used for mammography, and the reviewer says this should be referenced as a contraindication.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. The warning “Not to be used for mammography” should be referenced as contraindication.

evidence.claim: The reviewer requests reconsideration of RED Directive relevance for wireless signal transmission.
evidence.evidenceText: Please, reconsider relevancy to the RED Directive for the wireless transmission of the signals.

evidence.claim: The Product Description lists Basic UDI-DI 88000704HAAP-27U, but the reviewer asks for the UDI issuing entity.
evidence.evidenceText: Basic UDI-DI 88000704HAAP-27U Please, provide an issuing entity for the UDI code

evidence.claim: The reviewer says Korean regulatory requirement references should be removed because the medical device is being certified for the EU.
evidence.evidenceText: We are certifying the MD for EU. Please, remove the documentation reference to the Korean regulatory requirements

evidence.claim: The Product Description applies MDR Annex VIII Rule 17 as class IIa for devices intended to record diagnostic images generated by X-ray radiation.
evidence.evidenceText: Devices specifically intended for recording of diagnostic images generated by X-ray radiation are classified as class IIa

evidence.claim: The reviewer asks to reconsider MDR Annex VIII Rule 10, which classifies certain active diagnostic or therapeutic radiology devices as class IIb.
evidence.evidenceText: Please reconsider Rule 10 Active devices intended to emit ionizing radiation and intended for diagnostic or therapeutic radiology... are classified as class IIb.

evidence.claim: The reviewer requests use of MDCG 2020-5 for establishing clinical equivalence.
evidence.evidenceText: To establish equivalence, please, use MDCG 2020-5 Clinical Evaluation – Equivalence: A guide to manufactures and notified bodies

evidence.claim: The reviewer requests use of ISO 15223-1:2021 for labeling symbols.
evidence.evidenceText: Please, use current edition of ISO 15223-1:2021.

evidence.claim: The reviewer says the UDI carrier referenced in MDR Article 27(4) and Annex VI Part C should be on the label.
evidence.evidenceText: As per Section 23.2(h) the UDI carrier referred to in Article 27(4) and Part C of Annex should be on the label.

evidence.claim: The reviewer says other EU language versions of the user manual must be added to the technical documentation and translation procedure must ensure correct translations.
evidence.evidenceText: Any other EU language version of the User Manual must be added to the TD. The manufacturer must have a procedure which will ensure correct translation

evidence.claim: The reviewer says CE 2265 should be on the user manual title page and CE symbol is missing from the symbols table.
evidence.evidenceText: On the title page there should be the CE 2265 mark 1.2 Symbols CE symbol is missing in the table.

evidence.claim: The reviewer says CE 2265 does not need to be on the EU Declaration of Conformity if Notified Body Number 2265 is present.
evidence.evidenceText: The CE 2265 does not need to be on the DoC. It is sufficient to have the Notified Body Number: 2265.

evidence.claim: The reviewer says the EU Declaration of Conformity is missing the place for EUR certificate and date.
evidence.evidenceText: Missing is the place for EUR certificate and date

evidence.claim: The reviewer says common specifications should be listed under MDR, and standards used for MDR conformity may be referenced where CS are not specified.
evidence.evidenceText: As per MDR, it is necessary to list the CS (common specifications), when these are not yet specified, it is OK to refer to all standards used for establishing conformity with MDR requirements.

evidence.claim: The reviewer cites MDR 23.1(g) requiring residual risks communicated to users or others to be included as limitations, contraindications, precautions, or warnings.
evidence.evidenceText: MDR 23.1(g) Residual risks which are required to be communicated to the user and/or other person shall be included as limitations, contra-indications, precautions or warnings

evidence.claim: The manufacturing information states all three models are manufactured at 41-46, Cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-myeon, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, 17118, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: All three models are manufactured at 41-46, Cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-myeon, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do,17118, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: The reviewer identifies address inconsistencies between label, Product Description, Declaration of Conformity, and Manufacturing Information.
evidence.evidenceText: Please, check the address on the label against the address used in the Product Description... Please, check the address on the EU declaration of Conformity against the address used in the Product Description... Please, check the address on the label agains the address used in the Manufacturing information

evidence.claim: The reviewer says the Overseas Supplier Assessment document APF704-3 is missing.
evidence.evidenceText: Document Overseas Supplier Assessment (APF704-3) is missing in the provided documentation

evidence.claim: The GSPR checklist contains information on relevant standards, methods used, and references to supporting controlled documents required by Annex I of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: Checklist contains the information on relevant standards, methods used and references to supporting controlled documents as required by Annex I of the Regulation (EU) 2017/745
evidence.pageTarget: MDR General Safety and Performance Requirements Checklist

evidence.claim: The reviewer asks for supporting documents with revision status for the GSPR checklist.
evidence.evidenceText: Please, provide the supporting documents with the revision status (these are live documents which may be updated).

evidence.claim: The reviewer says ISO/TR 24971 should have a referenced edition.
evidence.evidenceText: ISO/TR 24971 should also have referenced edition.

evidence.claim: The reviewer says AQP712 Risk Management Regulations is not provided in the documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: Document AQP712 Risk Management Regulations not provided in the documentation

evidence.claim: The biocompatibility review says Appendix 3 for Polyester Pressure Sensitive Adhesive Tape ST-9108 is not provided.
evidence.evidenceText: Appendix 3 for Polyester Pressure Sensitive Adhesive Tape (ST-9108) is not provided.

evidence.claim: The Clinical Evaluation Plan should demonstrate progression from exploratory investigations to confirmatory investigations and PMCF under Annex XIV Part B.
evidence.evidenceText: The 9. Clinical development plan should not just be referring to PMCF, it should rather demonstrate - progression from exploratory investigations... to confirmatory investigations... and a PMCF as referred to in Part B of Annex XIV

evidence.claim: The reviewer says expected clinical benefits should be measurable outcome parameters under MDR definition 53.
evidence.evidenceText: the expected clinical benefit(s) (outcome parameters) should be measurable (MDR definition (53))

evidence.claim: The Clinical Evaluation Report states safety, benefit-risk ratio, performance by equivalence comparison, and acceptability of undesirable side effects are evaluated using literature review and clinical experience of similar devices.
evidence.evidenceText: to demonstrate safety and acceptable benefit-risk ratio... to evaluate performance... by comparison with equivalent device; and... demonstrate acceptability of undesirable side-effects... through evaluation of existing data of literature review and clinical experience of similar devices.

evidence.claim: The Clinical Evaluation Report says equivalence with three marketed products is demonstrated in the Equivalence Table of Section H.
evidence.evidenceText: Equivalence with 3 marketed products is demonstrated in the Equivalence Table of Section H.

evidence.claim: The PMCF Plan says no PMCF study is required for the digital X-ray flat panel detector because safety and clinical performance are already known from 20 years of previous use.
evidence.evidenceText: The PMCF study is not required for digital x-ray flat panel detector, because the safety and clinical performance are already known from previous use of the device in 20 years.

evidence.claim: The reviewer states that PMCF itself is necessary even if a PMCF study is not required.
evidence.evidenceText: The PMCF itself is necessary. As you state in Section G The PMCF study is not required for digital x-ray flat panel detector.

evidence.claim: The PMS Plan is the same for models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: PMS is the same for models A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The reviewer says the PMS Plan has no reference to threshold values and indicators for evaluating clinical benefits.
evidence.evidenceText: No reference to any threshold values and indicators to evaluate the clinical benefit(s), see MDR definition (53).

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal says MFDS/domestic, but the source text is primarily an EU MDR and CE technical documentation review and explicitly says Korean regulatory references should be removed for EU certification.
The NC report title provides date-like text '23.08.31', but no explicit full document date field is present.
No revision or version is provided for the NC report itself.
The source contains review comments from an NC report rather than finalized corrected technical documentation; claims should be treated as reviewer findings and requested actions.
Some extracted standards and regulatory references are cited without complete edition or clause details, including ISO/TR 24971 and some MDR annex references.
